pubmed_links: [Savas et al. (2015) The Sorting Receptor SorCS1 Regulates Trafficking of Neurexin and AMPA Receptors](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26291160/); [Ribeiro et al. (2019) SorCS1-mediated sorting in dendrites maintains neurexin axonal surface polarization required for synaptic function](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31658245/)
summary: SORCS1 encodes a sorting receptor that regulates trafficking of neurexin and AMPA receptors at synapses; loss of SorCS1 impairs presynaptic function and glutamatergic transmission, and SORCS1 mutations have been associated with autism and Alzheimer's disease. (emerging evidence)
